channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Travellers urged to take extra precautions as phone and jewellery thefts increase in Bali
item.description: Australian travellers planning a trip to Bali are being encouraged to remain vigilant following reports of an increase in pickpocketing, phone snatching and jewellery theft across several of the islan...

item.title: Newlywed Couple's Visit to Rigathi Gachagua's Home Focuses on Family Values
item.description: A newlywed couple, Charles Nguni Muriuki and Agnes Nduta Nguni, visited former deputy president Rigathi Gachagua's Wamunyoro home shortly after their wedding. The couple was welcomed by Gachagua and h...

item.title: Tinubu's Silence on Corruption Allegations Risks Institutional Trust
item.description: The article discusses the political and institutional risks posed by President Bola Tinubu's silence on corruption allegations involving his Chief of Staff, Femi Gbajabiamila, and Prince Adeniyi Adeye...

item.title: Opinion: FIFA criticised over decision to suspend Folarin Balogun's ban before Belgium match
item.description: This opinion article argues that FIFA severely damaged its credibility by suspending the one-match ban imposed on United States striker Folarin Balogun, allowing him to play in the World Cup round-of-...

item.title: Freight train derails in Repentigny, no injuries reported
item.description: A freight train derailed in Repentigny, Quebec, on Sunday afternoon, causing damage to infrastructure but no injuries. Police spokesperson Sgt. Bruno Marier confirmed that emergency calls were logged ...

item.title: Gigi Hadid and Bradley Cooper leave NYC after Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's wedding
item.description: Gigi Hadid and Bradley Cooper were among the high-profile guests at Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's lavish wedding at Madison Square Garden, which drew over 1,000 attendees including A-list celebritie...

item.title: Carolyn Hax Advises on Handling Betrayal in Divorce: Teaching Children About Love's Limits
item.description: The article discusses a letter writer's experience with the sudden end of a long-term marriage due to his wife's infidelity. The husband, along with their children, was blindsided by the betrayal, whi...

item.title: Ranchi's self-built settlement offers blind residents safety, community support and dignity amid everyday challenges
item.description: The article examines the lives of residents of Andra Basti, a self-established settlement in Ranchi created nearly three decades ago by blind people seeking safety, independence and mutual support. It...

item.title: 20-Year-Old Adam Ibrahim Tama Arrested in Katsina for Brutal Attack and Robbery on His Own Niece
item.description: The Katsina State Police Command don arrest one 20-year-old man wey dem call Adam Ibrahim Tama for armed robbery and attempted murder of him own niece. The incident happen on June 22, 2026, around 3:2...

item.title: Brazil's World Cup Exit: Norway's Victory Over Brazil in Round of 16
item.description: Brazil suffered a shocking defeat to Norway in the 2026 World Cup Round of 16, marking their earliest exit since 1990. The match at MetLife Stadium saw Norway's Erling Haaland score twice, securing vi...

item.title: Australia falls to Ireland in thrilling rugby match after missed penalty
item.description: In a high-stakes rugby clash between Australia and Ireland, the Wallabies suffered a narrow 33-31 defeat after a dramatic 10-try encounter. Despite an early lead and a blistering start, Australia's ho...

item.title: Simba Sports Club Dominates Match with Early Victory
item.description: Simba Sports Club showcased their dominance in a recent match at the Benjamin Mkapa Stadium, commonly known as Jangwani, by securing a commanding victory within the first 40 minutes. The team's tactic...

item.title: Iran's Supreme Leader Absent at Father's Funeral; Hormuz Shipping Fees Announced
item.description: The article covers multiple global events. Iran's Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei skipped his father's funeral, sparking speculation about his health after unconfirmed reports of injuries from strikes...

item.title: India's cricket team needs to adapt to different conditions: Kishan's insights
item.description: Ishan Kishan, the Indian wicketkeeper-batter, reflected on India's loss to England in the second T20I at Manchester, emphasizing the need for the team to better understand playing conditions. He ackno...

item.title: FIA Clarifies Safety Car Decision at British GP After Technical Glitch
item.description: The FIA addressed confusion surrounding the late deployment of a safety car during the British Grand Prix, clarifying that the decision adhered to regulations. The incident occurred when Max Verstappe...

item.title: Belgium Questions FIFA Ruling Allowing Suspended USA Forward Balogun to Play in Round of 16
item.description: My people, the Royal Belgian Football Association don shock plenty people as dem challenge FIFA ruling wey allow United States forward Folarin Balogun to play for their Round of 16 match against Belgi...

item.title: ICAC hearing raises serious questions about former La Trobe vice-chancellor John Dewar's conduct
item.description: The article is an opinion piece by Julie Hare examining the appearance of former La Trobe University vice-chancellor and former Universities Australia chair John Dewar before the NSW Independent Commi...

item.title: Norway's Viking Row Celebration Marks Historic World Cup Run
item.description: Norway's unexpected World Cup 2026 run has captured global attention, with their Viking Row celebration becoming a symbol of national pride. The team, led by Erling Haaland, reached the quarter-finals...

item.title: Analysis of Senator-Judges' Leanings in Sara Duterte's Impeachment Trial
item.description: The impeachment trial of Vice President Sara Duterte is being analyzed through the political strategies and affiliations of the senator-judges. Political analysts like Arjan Aguirre and Jean Franco hi...

item.title: Viljoens Face Legal Scrutiny as Wife Pledges Full Cooperation in Ongoing Investigations
item.description: Former celebrity lawyer Peet Viljoen faces 400 charges, including fraud and corruption, linked to an alleged R27 million property fraud scheme. His wife, Melanie Viljoen, has pledged full cooperation ...
